Petition To Remove Robert Weaver As Trustee And To Appoint Ronald H. Malone As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-19-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-06-289350 The Estate of Marshall I. Wais and PTR-08-291579 The Marshall I. Wais.

Appearances: Kyong Kim for Lonna Wais. Walter Cook for Judge Elaine Rushing (Trustee Ad Litem). Richard Collier for Marshall Wais, Jr., Peter Wais, Michael Wais and Laura Wais. Matthew Matiasevich for Robert Weaver (Trustee).

The Parties reported that they are here to resolve the Trust and that the related case is being resolved by way of a waiver of notice and approval of the accountings that were filed last week, that all of the Parties and their Attorneys have blessed it, that the Court is to assume the beginning balances, that they filed a petition to approve the settlement and dismiss the 850 petition, that they have initiated a new petition for approval of the settlement in a new trust case and termination of the Lonnamar Trust (PTR-14-298257) with a hearing date of February 18, 2015, and that they are requesting appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor Children. Counsel expressed that they want the matters resolved as soon as possible, that they would like the petition for breach of trust trailing, spoke of the accountings, termination of the Trusts and advancing hearing dates. Mr. Collier will file waivers and consents to the orders approving the settlements. Future dates are to be considered by the Court and were not set at the hearing.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Continued To Jun-08-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Petition To Remove Robert Weaver As Trustee And To Appoint Ronald H. Malone As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-12-2014 Continued To Nov-18-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-06-289350 The Estate of Marshall I. Wais and PTR-08-291579 the Marshall I. Wais Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Robert L. Farmer. Stacie Nelson and Nicole Neubert for Lonna Wais. Walter Cook for and with Judge Elaine Rushing (Trustee ad Litem). Richard Collier for (remaindermen) Marshall Wais Jr., Peter Wais, Michael Wais and Laura Wais (also present).

Mr. Collier reported that Ms. Wais and the 4 remaindermen reached a settlement, that it has been documented and signed with terms including the trust to be terminated, IRS procedures regarding marital dissolution being followed, release of all claims in the 850 petition, discharge of the Trustee ad Litem and that they would be meeting on Monday to iron out the last details. He also stated that they intend to file a petition for approval of the settlement agreement by mid October and would like implementation by the end of the year. Ms. Nelson reported that settlement with Mr. Weaver and the Farmers estate is still in the works, the remaindermen have agreed to waive notice, are in approval of the accountings in the trust case, that the objections of Mrs. Wais are being resolved with the settlement and that the Unitrust petition is to be dismissed. In addition, Mr. Collier clarified that all of this activity is in the trust matter, not the estate and that once the issues are resolved they will settle the estate. All parties expressed desire to have the petition for approval of settlement heard as early as possible. The court advised counsel that the earlier the petition gets filed the better and signed the stipulation for order staying discovery and tolling the 5 year statute for bringing the amended 850 petition to trial. Status on administration of estate was set for November 18, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition for removal in the trust matter is also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Court Reporter: Wendy Wachhorst, CSR #12290, 650-208-0405 Bailiff: Deputy R. McConoco. (Reported)

Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicole Neubert and Stacie Nelson for Lonna Wais. William Norman for and with Laura Wais (Remainderman). Walter Cook for and with Elaine Rusing (Trustee Ad Litem).
